summary.intent: Investigate and implement SQLite migrations for the CRM crate (CHA-10), adding four crm_* tables with proper schema, store implementations, and CLI migration support.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented all four crm_* tables (crm_contacts, crm_matters, crm_interactions, crm_contact_channels), extended the CrmStore trait and both SQLite/memory implementations, fixed CLI db_commands gaps, and passed all 45 CRM + 5 CLI tests with a clean PR created.

repo: The project uses a multi-crate Rust workspace with feature-gated crate integration in server.rs and cli/Cargo.toml
SQLite migrations use sqlx::migrate! with set_ignore_missing(true), allowing safe modification of existing init migrations when no production DBs exist
The crm_ prefix convention is enforced to avoid namespace collisions since multiple crates share the same moltis.db database
CLI db_commands.rs had a known gap: vault and CRM migrations were missing from both run_migrations() and clear_databases()
Pinned nightly toolchain is required for rustfmt — cargo fmt must use that toolchain explicitly
CLAUDE.md maintains a crate-to-table mapping that must be updated when new tables are added

code.finding: clear_databases() was missing vault and CRM tables — needed to extend the table list
run_migrations() was missing vault and CRM migration calls — both needed adding with cfg feature gates
All SQL queries referenced 'contacts' table name and needed updating to 'crm_contacts'
Single migration file handles all four tables in correct FK dependency order — simpler than separate files for a single crate

workflow: Rust cold builds are slow — implement all file changes before waiting for compile results to avoid blocking on build times
The Edit tool requires a prior Read in the same conversation; skipping the Read causes tool failures
Linear MCP integration (mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_execute) is the mechanism for posting workpad/investigation comments to Linear issues
Using cfg-gated feature flags in vec! macros is awkward — extending a base vec conditionally is cleaner
Running clippy per-crate (cargo clippy -p <crate>) is faster than workspace-wide checks during iterative development

friction: Rust cold builds took long enough that the agent had to proceed with implementation without confirming the baseline compiled clean
Write tool requires a prior Read — the agent hit an error trying to Write types.rs without re-reading it after the session context shifted
LINEAR_API_KEY was not available in the shell environment, requiring MCP plugin calls instead of direct API usage
cfg-gated table list in vec! was syntactically awkward, requiring a refactor to use extend() instead
Multiple Edit attempts on db_commands.rs (>3 edits) due to the complexity of adding cfg-gated code blocks cleanly
open_items: Schema design for crm_matters status enum values was not fully specified in the issue — chosen values may need revisiting
crm_contact_channels composite PK vs separate id decision was made without explicit issue guidance
Vault migrations were added to CLI db_commands.rs as part of this fix, but vault-specific testing of that path was not explicitly verified
Any existing developer databases with the old 'contacts' table name require a manual 'moltis db reset' — no migration path provided for existing dev environments
